content_en:
Zaid bin Harithah & answered:

“Father, | am deeply moved by the praiseworthy qualities of the character of the Prophet %, and by the way he treats me with love and affection. I just cannot leave him and live anywhere else.”

“O family of the Quraish, be witness that this is my son and heir, and ] am his heir.”

The father and uncle of Zaid bin Harithah 4 were surprised to hear this announcement and happy too. They went home pleased with the outcome. From that day on Zaid bin Harithah came to be called Zaid bin Muhammad, until such time as the Verses of the Noble Qur’an_ were revealed in where Allah Almighty commanded in Surah Al-Ahzab:

“Call them after their fathers: that is more just in the sight of

Then once again he began to be called, Zaid bin Harithah . OOK

When Zaid bin Harithah & gave preference to the Prophet % over his parents no one knew what wealth was in store for him. He did not even know to what position Muhammad % was to be elevated. No one knew that one day Muhammad 4 would be the Guide for the whole world, and a Divine Order would be established on the soil of Saudi Arabia, which would then become the cradle of Justice, Peace and

Tranquillity. Neither did Zaid bin Harithah % know that he would have the honour of being the first brick in the grand edifice of Islam. Doubtless this is due to the beneficence of Allah. He bestows on whom He Pleases.

Only a few years had passed after Zaid bin Harithah & had opted to stay with the Prophet 3s rather than with his parents, when Allah nominated the latter to the position of His Divine Messenger. He was to preach the Doctrine of the Truth and Unity or Oneness of Allah. Zaid bin Harithah 4 was the extremely fortunate man who first pledged allegiance to Islam personally to the Prophet %, believing in him. He had this unique honour and was the confidant of the Prophet # and one of the first Commanders-in-Chief of the army of Islam. He also had the honour of being the vicegerent of the Prophet 3 on an occasion when he had gone out of Al-Madinah to fight in a war.

Just as Zaid bin Harithah 4 had shown a great love for the Prophet 35 overriding all other bonds, so also the Prophet 3 considered him a son and truly a member of his family. Whenever Zaid bin Harithah 4 left on a mission he would pray earnestly for his success and be very happy to see him return.

“When Zaid bin Harithah & returned to Al-Madinah after a journey, the Prophet # happened to be in my house. When he knocked on the door, the Prophet 3 quickly got up to answer. He found that it was Zaid at the door; he was overjoyed and hugged him warmly, kissed his forehead. By Allah! I had never seen him so excited in welcoming any other Companion.”

This is why Zaid bin Harithah # was known among the Muslims as ‘Beloved of the Messenger of Allah’ and the son of Zaid bin Harithah 4 was known among the Companions as ‘Son of Beloved of the Messenger of Allah’.

In the year 8 Hijrah, Allah saw it fit to make His Prophet suffer the loss of a very loyal and beloved Companion. The Prophet 3 had sent Harith bin ‘Umair with a letter for the Shah of Basrah, inviting him to accept Islam. When he reached a place called Motah on the border of Jordan, Amir Ghassan Sharjeel bin ‘Amr confronted him, and then captured him and killed him. The Prophet 3 heard this terrible news with anguish and grief. He appointed Zaid bin Harithah # Commander-in-Chief and sent an army of three thousand soldiers to Motah. He said if Zaid bin Harithah # was martyred, then Ja’far bin Abi Talib # should take over the Command. In case he too was martyred, ‘Abdullah bin Rawahah & should become the Commander. And in case he too was martyred, the Mujahideen should choose whoever they considered the best to lead them.

The army of Islam set out reciting the Name of Allah and reached the eastern city of Ma‘an in Jordan. The Emperor of Rome came to the battlefield with a hundred thousand men; another hundred thousand ‘mushrikeen’ of Arabia joined up with him. This huge army came forward arrogantly and confronted the Muslims.

The Muslims spent two nights at Ma’an consulting and planning what military strategy should be adopted under the circumstances. One Mujahid suggested that they should write to the Prophet # informing him about the huge numbers of the opposing army, and wait for his orders.

Another said, “By Allah we do not fight depending on our numbers, neither do we fight depending on our military strength. We are armed only for the defence and protection of our True Faith. My companions! Remember the purpose for which we set out towards the battlefield of Jihad. Allah has guaranteed one of two prizes for you; either you will be blessed with victory or you will be blessed with martyrdom.”

At Motah the two opposing forces came face to face. There were only three thousand soldiers in the army of Muslims and there were two hundred thousand soldiers in the enemy army, The Mujahideen fought so courageously in the battle that the enemy panicked and was overcome.

He replied, “These are the tears of a beloved for a beloved.” In the Name of Allah, Most Gracious, Most Merciful

“Come back to your Lord - well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him).” (89: 28)

Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2 Prophet = supplicate for Jareer bin ‘Abdullah:

“O Allah % please grant Jareer bin ‘Abdullah the ability to sit firmly on his horse, and make him a righteous leader who will guide others to the straight path.”

Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2

Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir was a popular leader of the tribe of Bajeelah. He was a tall handsome and attractive man, and had a radiant face. This was his physical appearance; but by nature he was no less pleasing. He was a courteous, polite man with a superb command of the language and was soft-spoken yet eloquent. Considering all these wonderful traits of character, ‘Umar Faroog had given him the title of ‘A second Yusuf’

The Prophet %, in his Friday sermon, spoke of him in such a charming manner that everyone started to look at him enviously. This was at the time that he arrived at Al-Madinah and entered the mosque of the Prophet 3% to pledge allegiance to Islam.

Imam Ahmed in his Masnad describes the incident in the words of Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2 himself. He narrates:

“My trusted friends! A prince of Yemen will soon come in your midst, he will have an impressive regal look and dignity about him.”

My happiness knew no bounds when I heard these highly complimentary words used by the Prophet # for me. Doubtless this was a great honour coming as it did from the Prophet 3% himself. My heart seemed to sing and my face turned pink with pleasure and for the first time in my life 1 seemed to have found real happiness and peace. After the Friday prayers we presented ourselves before the Prophet %. He expressed his pleasure at seeing us so well dressed and spreading out his cloak for me asked me to be seated. He said,

“When a person from another tribe, deserving of respect comes to visit you, you must treat him with deference.”

Once my friends and I had settled down, he asked me affectionately, ’ How did you happen to come? We answered respectfully.

“O Messenger of Allah 38, we have come into your presence to pledge allegiance to Islam. We will be very fortunate indeed if we are blessed with the bounty of Islam.”

On hearing our request his face lit up. He told me:

“Come and swear the oath of allegiance to me and promise with all your heart and soul that there is no other worthy of worship but the One True Allah. And also acknowledge that I am the true Prophet sent by Allah. Besides this, you will have to offer the obligatory prayers regularly; paying ‘zakah’ or the poor dues is a must and you must fast during the month of Ramadhan. If you can afford it you have to perform the ‘Hajj’. You must wish well for all Muslims and behave sympathetically towards them. Remember the Allah has no Mercy on him who has no mercy on others. Co-operate with the person who has been appointed your leader or Amir, and obey him even if he is from a weaker tribe’”
content_id:
Zaid bin Harithah menjawab:

"Ayah, aku sangat tersentuh oleh sifat-sifat terpuji dari akhlak Nabi %, dan oleh cara beliau memperlakukanku dengan kasih sayang. Aku tidak sanggup meninggalkan beliau dan tinggal di tempat lain."

"Wahai keluarga Quraisy, saksikanlah bahwa ini adalah putraku dan ahli warisku, dan aku adalah ahli warisnya."

Ayah dan paman Zaid bin Harithah terkejut mendengar pernyataan ini dan juga merasa senang. Mereka pulang dengan gembira atas hasilnya. Sejak hari itu Zaid bin Harithah dikenal sebagai Zaid bin Muhammad, hingga ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an mulia diturunkan di mana Allah Yang Mahakuasa memerintahkan dalam Surah Al-Ahzab:

"Panggillah mereka (anak-anak angkat itu) dengan nama bapak-bapak mereka: itulah yang lebih adil di sisi

Maka sekali lagi ia mulai dipanggil Zaid bin Harithah.

Ketika Zaid bin Harithah lebih memilih Nabi % daripada kedua orang tuanya, tidak seorang pun tahu kekayaan apa yang tersimpan untuknya. Ia sendiri tidak tahu ke kedudukan apa Muhammad % akan diangkat. Tidak seorang pun tahu bahwa suatu hari Muhammad akan menjadi pembimbing bagi seluruh dunia, dan sebuah tatanan Ilahi akan ditegakkan di tanah Arab Saudi, yang kemudian akan menjadi buaian Keadilan, Kedamaian dan

Ketenangan. Zaid bin Harithah pun tidak tahu bahwa ia akan mendapat kehormatan menjadi batu bata pertama dalam bangunan megah Islam. Tidak diragukan lagi ini adalah karunia Allah. Dia memberikan kepada siapa yang Dia kehendaki.

Baru beberapa tahun berlalu setelah Zaid bin Harithah memilih tinggal bersama Nabi % daripada bersama orang tuanya, ketika Allah mengangkat beliau ke kedudukan sebagai Rasul-Nya. Beliau ditugaskan untuk menyerukan ajaran Kebenaran dan tawhid atau Keesaan Allah. Zaid bin Harithah adalah orang yang sangat beruntung yang pertama kali membai'at Islam secara langsung kepada Nabi %, dengan beriman kepadanya. Ia memiliki kehormatan yang unik ini dan merupakan orang kepercayaan Nabi % serta salah satu Panglima Tertinggi pertama pasukan Islam. Ia juga mendapat kehormatan menjadi wakil Nabi % pada suatu kesempatan ketika beliau pergi keluar Al-Madinah untuk berperang.

Sebagaimana Zaid bin Harithah telah menunjukkan kecintaan yang besar kepada Nabi % melebihi segala ikatan lainnya, demikian pula Nabi % menganggapnya sebagai putra dan benar-benar bagian dari keluarganya. Setiap kali Zaid bin Harithah berangkat dalam sebuah misi, beliau akan berdoa dengan sungguh-sungguh untuk keberhasilannya dan sangat gembira melihatnya kembali.

"Ketika Zaid bin Harithah kembali ke Al-Madinah setelah perjalanan, Nabi % kebetulan berada di rumahku. Ketika ia mengetuk pintu, Nabi % segera bangkit untuk membukanya. Beliau mendapati bahwa itu adalah Zaid di pintu; beliau sangat gembira dan memeluknya dengan hangat, mencium dahinya. Demi Allah! Aku belum pernah melihat beliau begitu bersemangat dalam menyambut sahabat lainnya."

Inilah sebabnya Zaid bin Harithah dikenal di kalangan kaum muslimin sebagai 'Kekasih Rasulullah' dan putra Zaid bin Harithah dikenal di kalangan para Sahabat sebagai 'Putra Kekasih Rasulullah'.

Pada tahun 8 Hijrah, Allah menakdirkan Nabi-Nya kehilangan seorang sahabat yang sangat setia dan tercinta. Nabi % telah mengutus Harith bin 'Umair dengan surat untuk penguasa Basrah, mengajaknya memeluk Islam. Ketika ia sampai di sebuah tempat bernama Mu'tah di perbatasan Yordania, Amir Ghassan Syarjeel bin 'Amr menghadangnya, kemudian menangkap dan membunuhnya. Nabi % mendengar berita mengerikan ini dengan kesedihan dan duka yang mendalam. Beliau mengangkat Zaid bin Harithah sebagai Panglima Tertinggi dan mengirim pasukan tiga ribu prajurit ke Mu'tah. Beliau bersabda jika Zaid bin Harithah gugur sebagai syahid, maka Ja'far bin Abi Talib harus mengambil alih komando. Jika ia pun gugur, 'Abdullah bin Rawahah harus menjadi komandan. Dan jika ia pun gugur, para mujahidin harus memilih siapa pun yang mereka anggap terbaik untuk memimpin mereka.

Pasukan Islam berangkat dengan menyebut nama Allah dan tiba di kota Ma'an di sebelah timur Yordania. Kaisar Romawi datang ke medan perang dengan seratus ribu pasukan; seratus ribu lagi kaum musyrikin Arab bergabung dengannya. Pasukan besar ini maju dengan sombong dan menghadapi kaum muslimin.

Kaum muslimin menghabiskan dua malam di Ma'an untuk bermusyawarah dan merencanakan strategi militer apa yang harus diterapkan dalam keadaan tersebut. Seorang mujahid mengusulkan agar mereka menulis surat kepada Nabi % untuk memberitahu beliau tentang besarnya jumlah pasukan lawan, dan menunggu perintah beliau.

Yang lain berkata, "Demi Allah, kita tidak berperang bergantung pada jumlah kita, kita pun tidak berperang bergantung pada kekuatan militer kita. Kita bersenjata hanya untuk membela dan melindungi agama kita yang benar. Wahai sahabat-sahabatku! Ingatlah tujuan yang karenanya kita berangkat menuju medan jihad. Allah telah menjamin salah satu dari dua hadiah untuk kalian; entah kalian diberkahi dengan kemenangan atau kalian diberkahi dengan kesyahidan."

Di Mu'tah kedua kekuatan yang berseberangan berhadapan muka. Hanya ada tiga ribu prajurit dalam pasukan muslimin dan ada dua ratus ribu prajurit dalam pasukan musuh. Para mujahidin bertempur dengan begitu gagah berani dalam pertempuran itu sehingga musuh panik dan kalah.

Beliau menjawab, "Ini adalah air mata seorang kekasih untuk kekasihnya." Dengan nama Allah Yang Maha Pengasih lagi Maha Penyayang

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha dan diridhai-Nya." (89: 28)

Jareer bin 'Abdullah bin Jabir. Nabi % mendoakan Jareer bin 'Abdullah:

"Ya Allah, karuniakanlah kepada Jareer bin 'Abdullah kemampuan untuk duduk dengan teguh di atas kudanya, dan jadikanlah ia pemimpin yang saleh yang akan membimbing orang lain ke jalan yang lurus."

Jareer bin 'Abdullah bin Jabir

Jareer bin 'Abdullah bin Jabir adalah seorang pemimpin terkenal dari suku Bajeelah. Ia adalah seorang pria yang tinggi, tampan dan menarik, serta berwajah cerah berseri. Itulah penampilan fisiknya; namun secara watak ia tidak kalah menyenangkan. Ia adalah seorang pria yang sopan dan santun dengan penguasaan bahasa yang luar biasa, bertutur lembut namun fasih. Mengingat semua sifat karakter yang luar biasa ini, 'Umar Farooq memberinya gelar 'Yusuf Kedua'.

Nabi %, dalam khutbah Jumatnya, berbicara tentangnya dengan cara yang begitu menawan sehingga semua orang mulai memandangnya dengan penuh kekaguman. Ini terjadi pada saat ia tiba di Al-Madinah dan memasuki masjid Nabi % untuk membai'at Islam.

Imam Ahmad dalam Musnad-nya menggambarkan peristiwa itu dalam kata-kata Jareer bin 'Abdullah bin Jabir sendiri. Ia meriwayatkan:

"Sahabat-sahabatku yang terpercaya! Seorang pangeran dari Yaman akan segera datang di tengah-tengah kalian, ia akan memiliki penampilan dan keagungan yang mengesankan."

Kebahagiaanku tak terhingga ketika mendengar kata-kata pujian yang tinggi dari Nabi % untukku. Tidak diragukan lagi ini adalah kehormatan besar yang datang langsung dari Nabi % sendiri. Hatiku seakan bernyanyi dan wajahku memerah karena senang, dan untuk pertama kalinya dalam hidupku aku merasa menemukan kebahagiaan dan ketenangan yang sejati. Setelah salat Jumat, kami menghadap Nabi %. Beliau menyatakan kegembiraannya melihat kami berpakaian begitu rapi dan menghamparkan jubahnya untukku lalu memintaku duduk. Beliau bersabda,

"Apabila seseorang dari suku lain yang layak dihormati datang mengunjungi kalian, kalian harus memperlakukannya dengan penuh penghormatan."

Setelah teman-temanku dan aku duduk dengan tenang, beliau bertanya kepadaku dengan penuh kasih sayang, 'Apa yang membawa kalian kemari?' Kami menjawab dengan penuh hormat.

"Wahai Rasulullah %, kami datang ke hadapan Anda untuk membai'at Islam. Kami akan sangat beruntung jika kami diberkahi dengan karunia Islam."

Mendengar permintaan kami, wajah beliau berseri-seri. Beliau berkata kepadaku:

"Kemarilah dan bersumpahlah membai'atku, dan berjanjilah dengan sepenuh hati dan jiwa bahwa tidak ada yang layak disembah selain Allah Yang Maha Esa. Dan akuilah pula bahwa aku adalah nabi sejati yang diutus oleh Allah. Selain itu, kamu harus menunaikan salat wajib secara teratur; membayar zakah adalah suatu keharusan dan kamu harus berpuasa selama bulan Ramadhan. Jika kamu mampu, kamu harus menunaikan haji. Kamu harus mendoakan kebaikan bagi semua muslim dan bersikap simpatik terhadap mereka. Ingatlah bahwa Allah tidak merahmati orang yang tidak menyayangi orang lain. Bekerjasamalah dengan orang yang telah ditunjuk sebagai pemimpin atau amir kalian, dan taatilah dia meskipun ia berasal dari suku yang lebih lemah."
content_ar: Zaid bin Harithah & answered: “الأب, | am deeply moved by the praiseworthy qualities of the الأخلاق of النبي ﷺ %, and by the الطريق he treats me with المحبة and affection. I just cannot leave him and live anywhere else.” “O الأسرة of the Quraish, be witness that this is my son and heir, and ] am his heir.” The الأب and uncle of Zaid bin Harithah 4 were surprised to hear this announcement and happy أيضاً. They went home pleased with the outcome. From that day on Zaid bin Harithah came to be called Zaid bin محمد ﷺ, حتى such time as the الآيات of the Noble Qur’an_ were revealed in where الله Almighty commanded in السورة Al-Ahzab: “Call them بعد their fathers: that is more just in the sight of Then حين again he began to be called, Zaid bin Harithah . OOK عندما Zaid bin Harithah & gave preference to النبي ﷺ % over his الوالدان no one knew what الثروة was in store for him. He did not even know to what position محمد ﷺ % was to be elevated. No one knew that يوماً ما محمد ﷺ 4 would be the Guide for the whole الدنيا, and a Divine Order would be established on the soil of Saudi Arabia, which would then become the cradle of العدل, Peace and Tranquillity. Neither did Zaid bin Harithah % know that he would have the honour of being the أولاً brick in the grand edifice of الإسلام. Doubtless this is بسبب the beneficence of الله. He bestows on whom He Pleases. Only a few years had passed بعد Zaid bin Harithah & had opted to stay with النبي ﷺ 3s rather than with his الوالدان, عندما الله nominated the latter to the position of His Divine Messenger. He was to preach the Doctrine of the الحق and Unity or Oneness of الله. Zaid bin Harithah 4 was the extremely fortunate man who أولاً pledged allegiance to الإسلام personally to النبي ﷺ %, believing in him. He had this unique honour and was the confidant of النبي ﷺ # and one of the أولاً القائدs-in-Chief of the الجيش of الإسلام. He أيضاً had the honour of being the vicegerent of النبي ﷺ 3 on an occasion عندما he had gone out of Al-المدينة المنورة to fight in a war. Just as Zaid bin Harithah 4 had shown a great المحبة for النبي ﷺ 35 overriding all other bonds, لذا أيضاً النبي ﷺ 3 considered him a son and truly a member of his الأسرة. كلما Zaid bin Harithah 4 left on a mission he would pray earnestly for his الفلاح and be very happy to see him return. “عندما Zaid bin Harithah & returned to Al-المدينة المنورة بعد a journey, النبي ﷺ # happened to be in my house. عندما he knocked on the door, النبي ﷺ 3 quickly got up to answer. He found that it was Zaid at the door; he was overjoyed and hugged him warmly, kissed his forehead. By الله! I had never seen him لذا excited in welcoming any other Companion.” This is why Zaid bin Harithah # was known among the المسلمون as ‘Beloved of الرسول ﷺ of الله’ and the son of Zaid bin Harithah 4 was known among the الصحابة as ‘Son of Beloved of الرسول ﷺ of الله’. In the year 8 Hijrah, الله saw it fit to make His النبي suffer the loss of a very loyal and beloved Companion. النبي ﷺ 3 had sent Harith bin ‘Umair with a letter for the Shah of Basrah, inviting him to accept الإسلام. عندما he reached a place called Motah on the border of Jordan, Amir Ghassan Sharjeel bin ‘Amr confronted him, and then captured him and killed him. النبي ﷺ 3 heard this terrible news with anguish and الحزن. He appointed Zaid bin Harithah # القائد-in-Chief and sent an الجيش of three thousand soldiers to Motah. قال إذا Zaid bin Harithah # was الشهيدed, then Ja’far bin Abi Talib # should take over the Command. In case he أيضاً was الشهيدed, ‘Abdullah bin Rawahah & should become the القائد. And in case he أيضاً was الشهيدed, the المجاهدين should choose whoever they considered the best to lead them. The الجيش of الإسلام set out reciting the Name of الله and reached the eastern city of Ma‘an in Jordan. The Emperor of Rome came to the المعركةfield with a hundred thousand men; another hundred thousand ‘mushrikeen’ of Arabia joined up with him. This huge الجيش came forward arrogantly and confronted the المسلمون. The المسلمون spent two nights at Ma’an consulting and planning what military strategy should be adopted under the circumstances. One Mujahid suggested that they should write to النبي ﷺ # informing him about the huge numbers of the opposing الجيش, and wait for his orders. Another said, “By الله we do not fight depending on our numbers, neither do we fight depending on our military القوة. We are armed only for the defence and protection of our True الإيمان. My الصحابة! Remember the purpose for which we set out towards the المعركةfield of الجهاد. الله has guaranteed one of two prizes for you; either you will be blessed with النصر or you will be blessed with الشهيدdom.” At Motah the two opposing forces came face to face. There were only three thousand soldiers in the الجيش of المسلمون and there were two hundred thousand soldiers in the العدو الجيش, The المجاهدين قاتل لذا courageously in the المعركة that the العدو panicked and was overcome. أجاب, “These are the tears of a beloved for a beloved.” In the Name of الله, Most Gracious, Most Merciful “Come back to your Lord - well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him).” (89: 28) Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2 النبي = supplicate for Jareer bin ‘Abdullah: “O الله % please grant Jareer bin ‘Abdullah the ability to sit firmly on his horse, and make him a الصالح leader who will guide others to the straight الطريق.” Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2 Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir was a popular leader of the tribe of Bajeelah. He was a tall handsome and attractive man, and had a radiant face. This was his physical appearance; but by nature he was no less pleasing. He was a courteous, polite man with a superb command of the language and was soft-spoken yet eloquent. Considering all these wonderful traits of الأخلاق, ‘عمر Faroog had given him the title of ‘A ثانياً يوسف ﷺ’ النبي ﷺ %, in his Friday sermon, spoke of him in such a charming manner that everyone started to look at him enviously. This was at the time that he arrived at Al-المدينة المنورة and entered the المسجد of النبي ﷺ 3% to pledge allegiance to الإسلام. الإمام Ahmed in his Masnad describes the incident in the words of Jareer bin ‘Abdullah bin Jabir 2 himself. He narrates: “My trusted friends! A prince of Yemen will soon come in your midst, he will have an impressive regal look and الكرامة about him.” My happiness knew no bounds عندما I heard these highly complimentary words used by النبي ﷺ # for me. Doubtless this was a great honour coming as it did from النبي ﷺ 3% himself. My القلب seemed to sing and my face turned pink with pleasure and for the أولاً time in my الحياة 1 seemed to have found real happiness and peace. بعد the Friday الصلوات we presented ourselves قبل النبي ﷺ %. He expressed his pleasure at seeing us لذا well dressed and spreading out his cloak for me asked me to be seated. قال, “عندما a person from another tribe, deserving of الاحترام comes to visit you, you must treat him with deference.” حين my friends and I had settled down, he asked me affectionately, ’ How did you happen to come? We answered respectfully. “O Messenger of الله 38, we have come into your presence to pledge allegiance to الإسلام. We will be very fortunate indeed إذا we are blessed with the bounty of الإسلام.” On hearing our request his face lit up. He told me: “Come and swear the oath of allegiance to me and promise with all your القلب and النفس that there is no other worthy of worship but the One True الله. And أيضاً acknowledge that I am the true النبي sent by الله. بالإضافة إلى this, you will have to offer the obligatory الصلوات regularly; paying ‘الزكاة’ or the poor dues is a must and you must fast خلال the month of Ramadhan. إذا you can afford it you have to perform the ‘الحج’. You must wish well for all المسلمون and behave sympathetically towards them. Remember the الله has no الرحمة on him who has no الرحمة on others. Co-operate with the person who has been appointed your leader or Amir, and obey him even إذا he is from a weaker tribe’”
